hypotheses.hypothesis: Frequency and semantic features dynamically govern compound processing at different time stages

title: Beyond localized methane plume detection: a dual-path deep learning framework for sensor-agnostic global hyperspectral methane plume monitoring
task: Sensor-agnostic hyperspectral methane plume detection
key_results: Dual-path (ENH/RAD) framework reduces latency vs. traditional processing; ENH higher accuracy; RAD avoids preprocessing; explainable AI validates physics-grounded learning
comments: Cross-sensor generalization (EMIT, Tanager-1) is strong; interpretability via SHAP/LIME enhances scientific validity.

hypotheses.result: ENH higher segmentation accuracy; RAD faster with no preprocessing; SHAP reveals physics-consistent feature learning; robust across EMIT and Tanager-1

title: Machine learning global atomic representations with Euclidean fast attention

key_results: Euclidean fast attention (EFA) achieves linear complexity while capturing long-range chemical interactions; enables MLFFs to model interactions conventional methods fail on
comments: Novel positional encoding preserves physical symmetries; significant efficiency gains but limited experimental validation on diverse chemical systems.

model_io.model: Euclidean fast attention (EFA)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Linear-scaling attention with Euclidean rotary encoding captures long-range interactions as effectively as quadratic attention

title: A causal discovery and inference framework for on-demand food delivery delays

key_results: 16.7% of 405,180 orders delayed; pickup/transport duration strongest causal drivers; delay propagation quantified; root-cause insights for platform optimization
comments: Bayesian causal discovery + double ML combination is sound; addresses practical industry problem; limited to China dataset, geographic generalization uncertain.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pickup/transport duration and delay propagation are true causal drivers, not mere correlations

title: A compact digital compute-in-memory Ising annealer with probabilistic SRAM bit in 28 nm for travelling salesman problem
task: Compact digital compute-in-memory Ising annealer for combinatorial optimization
key_results: 28 nm CMOS implementation; 96-city TSP solution in 620 µs at 961 nJ; 15–572× improvement in hardware cost ratio vs. prior solvers
comments: Co-optimized algorithm-hardware approach leveraging process variations for probabilistic bits is clever; scalability beyond 96-city TSP not demonstrated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical clustering + compact weight mapping + SRAM process variation enable energy-efficient Ising annealing on CMOS

title: A corn shaped ultrasonic meta-buffer rod design for online temperature monitoring in additive manufacturing
task: Online high-temperature monitoring in FDM via metamaterial-based ultrasonic buffer rod
key_results: Accurate surface temperature recording up to 190 °C; 60.9% mass reduction vs. conventional designs; reliable real-time monitoring during FDM

title: Functional hierarchy of the human neocortex across the lifespan
task: Map lifespan trajectories of large-scale functional connectivity gradients in human neocortex
key_results: Three dominant gradients show nonlinear developmental trajectories: infancy anchored by sensory systems, differentiation during childhood/adolescence, dedifferentiation with aging; gradient metrics predict cognitive performance across development
comments: Comprehensive normative reference of major value, but transcriptomic-functional coupling weakening with age may reflect reduced organizational precision or technical confounds.

model_io.model: Human neocortex resting-state fMRI and transcriptomics across lifespan (birth-100 years)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Functional gradients follow static adult organizational principles across lifespan
hypotheses.result: Rejected; gradients show complex nonlinear developmental trajectories with differentiation then dedifferentiation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gradient architecture predicts cognitive performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; gradient metrics correlate with behavioral outcomes across development

title: Androgen activity in the male embryonic hindbrain drives lethal PFA ependymoma
task: Determine role of androgen signaling in sex-biased PFA ependymoma tumorigenesis
key_results: Male hindbrain gliogenic progenitors show reduced differentiation; androgen signaling (not sex chromosomes) delays differentiation; androgen supplementation promotes PFA growth; androgen blockade reduces stem-like potential and proliferation
comments: Four-core genotype model elegantly separates chromosomal from gonadal contributions, though cell-type specificity of androgen effects within hindbrain remains to be resolved.

model_io.model: Four-core genotype mouse hindbrain development
Orthotopic PFA ependymoma with androgen manipulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sex chromosome composition drives male-female differences in PFA ependymoma
hypotheses.result: Rejected; androgen signaling drives sex differences, not chromosomal effects

hypotheses.hypothesis: Androgen signaling delays hindbrain differentiation and promotes tumorigenesis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; androgen-dependent effects observed in normal and tumor contexts

title: Rapid concerted switching of the neural code in the inferotemporal cortex
task: Characterize rapid dynamic switching of neural tuning codes in face-selective cortex
key_results: Face-selective neurons in inferotemporal cortex switch from object-general detection code to face-specific discrimination code in <20 ms; response gradients reverse and new high-dimensional tuning emerges
comments: Remarkable temporal precision and specificity, but the functional advantage of such rapid switching over sequential processing stages remains unclear.

model_io.model: Macaque inferotemporal cortex single-unit recordings during face and object presentation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Face-selective cells use a unified object-general code for all stimuli
hypotheses.result: Rejected; cells rapidly transition to face-specific code only for faces

hypotheses.hypothesis: Neural code switches from detection to discrimination via concerted population event
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; <20 ms transition observed specifically for face stimuli

title: Ectopic NMDAR expression in cancer unmasks germline-encoded autoimmunity
task: Establish link between ectopic tumor NMDAR expression and anti-NMDAR autoimmune encephalitis
key_results: NMDAR expression in TNBC induces affinity-matured anti-NMDAR antibodies from germline precursors; matured antibodies target specific epitopes and cause autonomic dysregulation and seizure threshold lowering in mice
comments: Mechanistically elegant connection between cancer and paraneoplastic syndrome, though human disease causality remains inferential.

model_io.model: Orthotopic TNBC tumors with inducible GluN1-GluN2B NMDARs
Passive antibody transfer in healthy female mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intratumoural NMDAR expression is sufficient to induce anti-NMDAR antibody maturation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; affinity-matured antibodies emerged from pre-existing germline precursors

hypotheses.hypothesis: Antibodies induce distinct functional effects on NMDARs (inhibition vs. potentiation)
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; conformational rearrangements predict variable functional outcomes

title: Parasites trigger epithelial cell crosstalk to drive gut–brain signalling
task: Elucidate paracrine epithelial signaling mechanisms linking parasitic infection to gut-brain protective behaviors
key_results: Tuft cells use two ACh release modes (acute parasite-metabolite-triggered and sustained leak-like during type 2 inflammation); sustained ACh activates EC cell serotonin release sufficient to suppress food intake via vagal afferent neurons
comments: Two-phase model elegantly explains symptom progression from asymptomatic to established disease, though the behavioral ecology rationale for food suppression during parasitemia could be clearer.

model_io.model: Intestinal epithelial tuft and enterochromaffin cells during parasitic infection

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tuft and EC cells communicate via paracrine signaling to coordinate neuro-immune responses
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; muscarinic receptor activation of EC cells by tuft-released ACh triggers serotonin release

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sustained ACh release (not acute release) elicits sufficient serotonin for behavioral effects
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; only constitutive leak-like ACh mode reaches serotonin levels sufficient to suppress food intake

title: Aversive learning hijacks a brain sugar sensor to consolidate memory
task: Determine how brain sugar sensors consolidate aversive memories independent of feeding
key_results: Spaced aversive training disinhibits fructose-sensing neurons into a fasted state; post-learning sugar intake activates these neurons to trigger thyrostimulin release and memory consolidation
comments: Elegant cross-talk between metabolic and cognitive circuits, though the behavioral readout (sucrose preference) may conflate emotional eating with genuine memory consolidation strength.

model_io.model: Drosophila aversive learning (spaced training) and appetitive learning

hypotheses.hypothesis: Brain fructose sensors contribute to memory consolidation in both appetitive and aversive contexts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; disinhibition mechanism restores sensing ability in satiated flies during spaced training

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thyrostimulin release from activated fructose neurons triggers memory consolidation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; post-learning sugar ingestion activates these neurons to consolidate memories

title: Intrinsically stretchable organic light-emitting-diode with high brightness and stretchability via elastic-microphase-engineered emitter and dual-embedded electrode

model_io.model: Intrinsically stretchable OLED stack
